lead.h1: U.S. declines to renew USMCA, opening a 10-year annual-review cycle
lead.context: The pact stays in force but is now renegotiated every year through 2036. It is the biggest overhang on the peso, on nearshoring investment, and on autos, the whole $1.8T relationship is an open question again.

items.headline: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured
items.context: Postpones the pending federal and local judicial elections from 2027 to 2028 and reshapes the Court (rotating two-year presidency, two sections). A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election. Secondary laws are moving through Congress.

items.headline: Moody's cuts Mexico to Baa3, the last investment-grade rung
items.context: Chiefly the cost of propping up Pemex plus structural deficits, the credit story of the moment. Standing ratings: Moody's Baa3 stable, S&P BBB negative, Fitch BBB- stable.

items.headline: June inflation cools to 3.37%, lowest since December 2020
items.context: A third straight monthly decline, back inside Banxico's 3%±1% band. This is the print that underwrites the pause and keeps real rates high, supporting the peso.

items.headline: Mexico to file U.S. criminal complaints over citizens' deaths in immigration enforcement
items.context: The sharpest escalation yet in the confrontation with Washington over the deportation crackdown, Mexico will pursue complaints against those responsible for 14 deaths in ICE custody and civil suits against private detention operators.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.48 to the dollar, inflation is 3.37%, the policy rate is 6.50%.

live.tmpl: the peso trades at {v} to the dollar
